title: RentCompass Phase 2 benchmark case
description: One offline task-benchmark case. Validated by evaluation/benchmark/validate.py. Tool names must be real registry tools or documented pseudo-routes; see README.md.

case_id.description: Unique id. Prefix is the category: A-H, or CR for the cold-resilience shard (cases_cold_resilience.jsonl). H = guard-regression shard (cases_guard_regression.jsonl).

user_query.description: The current-turn user message the agent must answer.

conversation_history.description: Prior turns that set up multi-turn / memory state. May be empty.

user_id.description: Identity used for per-user memory isolation. Distinct ids => distinct memory buckets.

expected_tools.description: Real registry tools OR documented pseudo-routes the agent SHOULD use. Empty array = a pure no-tool answer is acceptable.

forbidden_tools.description: Tools/routes the agent MUST NOT use for this case.

expected_constraints.description: Machine-checkable constraint objects. Vocabulary of 'type' is documented in README.md.

expected_grounding_sources.description: Where every factual claim in a correct answer must be traceable to (tool/data source or the user's own stated figures).

reference_calculations.description: Ground-truth arithmetic. Each entry pairs a human-readable formula string with its numeric result.

failure_conditions.description: Plain-language descriptions of what makes an answer WRONG for this case.

notes.description: Every assumption made (deposit weeks, money formula, move-in cost composition, etc.).

smoke.description: True for the small diverse subset run first, before any paid full run.

expected_route.description: The dominant route the agent's supervisor should take (a tool name or a pseudo-route).

allowed_tool_paths.description: OPTIONAL. Path-based expected trace for --arch fc_loop grading. A list of ALLOWED paths; a case matches if the actual tool trace equals ANY one of them. Each path is an ordered list of BATCHES; each batch is a list of tool names executed concurrently in one agent super-step. Batch-internal order is INSIGNIFICANT (compared as a set); cross-batch order IS significant. An empty batch list [] means 'no tools' (pure answer/refusal); [[]] means the single allowed path is the empty trace. Tool names must be real registry tools, the new fc_loop tools (ask_user, compare_or_rank_areas), or documented pseudo-routes. For --arch legacy this field is ignored and the expected_tools/forbidden_tools subset semantics apply instead.

items.description: One allowed path: an ordered sequence of concurrent batches.
One batch: tool names run concurrently in a single super-step (order insignificant).

hard_gate.description: OPTIONAL. If true, this case is an individually-mandatory gate: it must pass on its own and failures are reported by case_id, never averaged into an aggregate pass_rate. Used by the guard-regression shard (category H_guard_regression) where each case reproduces one historical bug / deleted deterministic guard.

fixture.description: Recorded tool-output fixture(s) under fixtures/ that the runner replays as tool evidence for this case.
